name: Fleetbase
researched_as: Fleetbase logistics and fleet operations platform
what: Open-source logistics/fleet management platform for dispatch, tracking, operations, and extensible fleet workflows.
solves: Driver/fleet operations, dispatch, route and order coordination, vehicle/resource oversight, and logistics extensions.
users: Logistics operators, delivery businesses, and internal fleet teams.
fit: Conditional. Useful only if Hylono runs meaningful delivery/installation/field-service logistics beyond simple shipment handling.
runtime: Laravel/PHP platform with modular extensions, database, web app, API, and infrastructure assets including Docker Compose and Helm.
components: Core platform, extensions, DB, admin UI, APIs, and logistics entities such as vehicles/drivers/orders.
deploy: Self-hosting appears well-supported in repo assets (compose, Helm, Caddy). Still only justified if logistics becomes a true internal capability.
deps: Database, storage, mail, reverse proxy, scheduler/jobs, mapping/tracking integrations depending on use case.
auth: Local roles/users and API access; verify SSO options if needed.
apis: Platform APIs and extension surfaces for logistics workflows.
security: May include driver/operator data, location data, and customer order context. Strong access control and data minimization are necessary.
setup_path: Start from repo blueprints only after confirming a real logistics use case; otherwise keep it off the active roadmap.
env: DB settings, app URL, mail, storage, map/tracking provider keys, scheduler/job config, and reverse-proxy settings.
persistence: Back up DB and any operational document storage; location/event data retention needs policy.
operations: Extension compatibility, dispatch workflow quality, mobile/operator UX, and data retention all require attention.
failure_points: Deploying it without a real fleet/logistics domain, or overlapping responsibilities with Leihs, CMMS, and standard shipping tooling.
integration: Could connect to rental deliveries, service visits, and field operations if Hylono grows an internal fleet/service organization.
verify: Confirm whether Hylono actually has or plans a managed delivery/install fleet. If not, Fleetbase is likely unnecessary.
risk: High redundancy risk unless logistics is core.
gdpr: Location and operator data increase privacy sensitivity.
recommendation: LATER / ONLY IF FLEET OPERATIONS BECOME REAL
overlap: Overlaps partially with Leihs/CMMS for operational assets and with generic dispatch tools.
codex_read_first: Read the Hylono operating model before touching Fleetbase.
codex_best_path: Do not implement unless a real dispatch/fleet use case is confirmed.
codex_never_assume: Never assume shipping or rental logistics automatically justify a fleet platform.

assets.name: Helm assets in repo
assets.notes: Useful if K8s deployment is ever needed.

sources.why: Product positioning and docs entry point.
